query: How active is the 2026 US tornado season and what is the forecast/outlook for early July 2026 severe weather? Current ENSO state (La Nina/El Nino) for summer 2026 and any Climate Prediction Center outlook for July 2026 severe convective weather in northern Plains and Midwest.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Important correction to the premise:** Summer 2026 is in an **emerging El Niño** (not La Niña). CPC reports El Niño conditions have developed (Niño-3.4 +0.7°C per CPC, up to +1.7°C per IRI), following a La Niña→El Niño transition in spring that actually suppressed the May severe season.

**2026 season status:** Above average overall (~1,051 reports through June 21), but with a distinctive pattern — a very active March (204 confirmed, 3rd most on record), a sharply suppressed May (only 119, well below the ~265 average), and a strong June rebound (244+) centered in the Midwest. **Illinois is the surprise national leader** with a record state year.

**Early July 2026 — ACTIVE:** SPC outlooks show repeated severe weather in the **northern Plains/Upper Midwest** in a classic northwest-flow "ring of fire" regime along the north edge of a heat dome:
- Day 1-3 (Jun 29–Jul 1): Slight/Marginal Risks with very large hail, damaging winds (75+ mph), isolated tornadoes
- Day 4-8 (Jul 2-7): "Repeated days of convective activity" with MCS development Nebraska into the Dakotas on July 2-3 and possibly July 4

By the week of **July 6-12**, the CPC Week-2 outlook becomes **heat-dominated** (extreme heat across central/eastern CONUS), with no explicit severe-convective hazard — implying severe risk narrows to the far northern tier. The dominant severe mode is damaging-wind/hail MCS events, not a high-end tornado outbreak.

The full structured findings with all sources and data points have been recorded.

sources.title: Climate Prediction Center: ENSO Diagnostic Discussion
sources.snippet: El Niño conditions developed over the past month… latest weekly Niño-3.4 index value was +0.7°C… 63% chance of a very strong El Niño during November-January. Next discussion 9 July 2026.

sources.title: IRI ENSO Forecast (June 22, 2026)
sources.snippet: El Niño conditions strengthening; Niño-3.4 reached +0.94°C in May 2026; latest weekly index (June 17) +1.7°C.

sources.title: List of United States tornadoes from May to June 2026
sources.snippet: May well below average (119 confirmed); June above average (244+ confirmed) with Illinois/Indiana outbreaks June 11, 17, 21; La Niña to El Niño transition suppressed May.

sources.title: List of United States tornadoes from January to March 2026
sources.snippet: Jan 21, Feb 49, Mar 204 confirmed — March 3rd most active on record behind 2022 and 2025.

sources.title: SPC Day 4-8 Convective Outlook (issued Jun 29, 2026, valid Jul 2-7)
sources.snippet: Repeated days of convective activity; MCS development Nebraska into Dakotas Thursday and Friday (Jul 2-3); another round possible Saturday (Jul 4) in Upper Midwest.

sources.title: SPC Day 1-3 Convective Outlooks (Jun 29, 2026)
sources.snippet: Slight Risk N Plains/Upper Midwest Jun 29 (very large hail, 75+ mph gusts, isolated tornadoes); Slight Risk N Wisconsin/Upper MI Jun 30; Marginal Risk Jul 1.

sources.title: CPC US Hazards Outlook (valid Jul 6-12, 2026)
sources.snippet: Extreme heat dominant hazard across central/eastern CONUS week-2; moderate/slight heat risks Jul 6-10; high winds CA/PNW; no severe-convective hazard designated.

sources.title: Illinois Leads the Nation: 2026 U.S. Tornado Count
sources.snippet: Illinois leads nation in 2026 tornado reports; ~210 preliminary reports, 170 confirmed.

sources.title: Illinois record tornadoes for the year in June (Fox Weather)
sources.snippet: Illinois averages ~50 tornadoes/yr; recorded triple that by June 2026.

data_points.metric: Weekly Niño-3.4 SST anomaly (IRI, week of June 17)
Chance of very strong El Niño Nov-Jan 2026-27
Week-2 heat index forecast (central/eastern CONUS, Jul 6-12)
